scientific_scope: Prospective validation of classical observable headroom in a disclosed dedicated-shuttle extension of Program O. This contract does not establish neural value or authorize Paper 3.

thesis_native: Op9, Op10 and Op12 have daily freight rate ROP=24h
Op10 and Op12 each have 24h processing time
distribution-vehicle availability and route planning are taken for granted
all orders traverse the physical Op10-Op12 tandem path before completion
researcher_introduced: one dedicated downstream mission is committed at every daily clock slot whether loaded or empty
each mission reserves 24 vehicle-and-crew hours at Op10 plus 24 at Op12
each mission reserves capacity for 2600 rations whether used or idle
loaded payload is utilization of the reserved mission, not an additional purchased resource
not_claimed: the thesis explicitly states empty running
the real MFSC pays a flat fixed freight price
the two synthetic product classes numerically calibrate the 21 real ration types
intervention_ledger.falsification_branch: Under loaded-only or pay-per-use accounting, Program O fails the resource gate and remains a boundary result.

primary_policy.selection_reason: shorter three-week horizon and parsimony among the two development-passing belief-MPC configurations
observations: strictly prior product-request labels through the frozen belief
backlog quantity count and maximum age by product

open_loop_comparator.definition: all base-4 length-eight calendars under centered_minority_v1
open_loop_comparator.selected_on: the existing burned development block only
open_loop_comparator.bootstrap_rule: reselect the strongest open-loop calendar inside every paired bootstrap resample

opening_forbidden_until: contract and source hashes are recorded

guardrails_noninferior: omitted and unresolved orders and quantity
action_certificate: at least two actions with ten-percent support
modal fixed and phase-only replacements strictly underperform

secondary_resource_estimand.name: pay_per_use
secondary_resource_estimand.claim_limit: If actual loaded trips, payload or loaded vehicle-hours are treated as purchased resources, the development result fails and no pay-per-use claim is allowed.
